name: From Oliveira do Bairro to Mortágua
name_original: De Oliveira do Bairro a Mortágua — percurso circular
description: This difficult 47.0-mile (75.6 km) road cycling route from Oliveira do Bairro to Mortágua offers challenging climbs and rural scenery.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Oliveira do Bairro, located in Portugal's Aveiro District, traverse a landscape of gentle rolling hills and agricultural fields. The region offers a mix of flat stretches and moderate climbs, providing varied terrain for road cyclists. Proximity to the Ria de Aveiro lagoon allows for extensive flat cycling paths, while the Cértima River's floodplains also present relatively level routes. This combination of features contributes to diverse road cycling opportunities. Best road cycling routes around Oliveira Do Bairro The most popular road cycling route is From Oliveira do Bairro to Mortágua, a 47.0 miles (75.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 54 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain across its length. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Oliveira do Bairro to Palhaça — circular route, an easy 17.0 miles (27.4 km) path. This circular route offers a gentler ride through the local countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Pateira de Fermentelos from Oliveira do Bairro — circular route, a 24.4 miles (39.3 km) trail leading through agricultural areas and near the Pateira de Fermentelos, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Road cycling around Oliveira Do Bairro is defined by gentle rolling hills, flat paths near the Ria de Aveiro, and routes along the Cértima River floodplains. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult rides, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Oliveira Do Bairro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Oliveira Do Bairro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Oliveira do Bairro?
A: Oliveira do Bairro offers a diverse selection of over 35 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and challenging options, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Oliveira do Bairro?
A: The region features a mix of gentle rolling hills and agricultural fields, providing varied terrain. You'll find both flat stretches, particularly near the Ria de Aveiro lagoon and along the Cértima River floodplains, as well as moderate climbs in other areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Oliveira do Bairro?
A: Given the Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers and mild, wet winters, spring and autumn are generally ideal for road cycling in Oliveira do Bairro. The temperatures are pleasant, and the landscapes are vibrant.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Oliveira do Bairro?
A: Yes, there are several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383648469/from-oliveira-do-bairro-to-palhaca-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Oliveira do Bairro to Palhaça — circular route&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 17.0-mile (27.4 km) path offering a gentler ride through the local countryside.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383634070/from-oliveira-do-bairro-to-mortagua" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Oliveira do Bairro to Mortágua&lt;/a&gt; provide a difficult 47.0-mile (75.6 km) journey with significant elevation gain across its length.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Oliveira do Bairro has several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383654516/pateira-de-fermentelos-from-oliveira-do-bairro-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pateira de Fermentelos from Oliveira do Bairro — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, a 24.4-mile (39.3 km) trail that leads through agricultural areas and near the Pateira de Fermentelos.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Oliveira do Bairro?
A: Many routes offer views of the region's natural beauty. You can cycle near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6031561" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pateira de Espinchel&lt;/a&gt;, a natural area with walkways and viewpoints, or along the Cértima River. The proximity to the Ria de Aveiro lagoon also means access to extensive flat paths with diverse ecosystems.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the road cycling routes?
A: While specific viewpoints on road cycling routes are not detailed, the region's gentle rolling hills and proximity to natural areas like the Pateira de Espinhel offer picturesque vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6315591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pateira de Requeixo Swing&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6955156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pateira de Fermentelos Boardwalk and Viewing Platform&lt;/a&gt; are notable spots for wide views of the lagoon.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Oliveira do Bairro?
A: The road cycling routes in Oliveira do Bairro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. Cyclists often appreciate the varied terrain, from flat paths to moderate climbs, and the scenic agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Oliveira do Bairro?
A: Yes, the region offers easy routes that are suitable for families. The flatter sections, particularly those near the Ria de Aveiro lagoon or along the Cértima River, provide safer and more enjoyable rides for cyclists of all ages.

Q: Can I find parking near the road cycling routes?
A: While specific parking details for every route are not provided, areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4614566" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alta Vila Municipal Park&lt;/a&gt; are known for parking facilities and can serve as convenient starting points for exploring nearby routes.

Q: Are there options for longer distance road cycling routes?
A: Yes, beyond the immediate municipality, Oliveira do Bairro's central location allows for longer rides. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383634070/from-oliveira-do-bairro-to-mortagua" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Oliveira do Bairro to Mortágua&lt;/a&gt;, at 47.0 miles (75.6 km), offer a substantial distance for those looking for extended rides.

Q: Are there any routes that offer stops for refreshments or cafes?
A: While specific cafes along routes are not listed, many routes pass through or near villages and towns within Oliveira do Bairro and its surroundings. These areas typically offer local cafes and restaurants where cyclists can stop for refreshments.
tips: The Oliveira do Bairro Parish Church, built in the 17th century and renovated in the 19th century, stands out for its baroque interior with gilded altarpieces and sacred images, including Saint Michael the Archangel and the Virgin of the Rosary.
Ribeira de Montágua begins in the village of the same name, Montágua, and flows into the Mondego River. Next to the bridge is a small river beach.
Stopping point very close to the Convent of Santa Cruz do Buçaco. It has a water spout for refilling, as well as a table with concrete benches.

The main square in Anadia is named after António Luís de Seabra, the Viscount of Seabra. Despite his connection with Anadia, the Viscount was born on board a ship that sailed near Cape Verde. In addition to a statue in his honor, the square houses Anadia's court.
Beautiful little chapel in Oliveira do Bairro. It is close to the new cemetery and can be easily seen as you walk along the street.

faqs.question: How difficult is the road cycling route from Oliveira do Bairro to Mortágua?
faqs.answer: This route is considered difficult or hard for road cycling. It requires very good physical condition due to its significant elevation gain of approximately 1259 meters over 75.6 kilometers. It's best suited for experienced cyclists looking for a challenging ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly on mostly paved surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. However, be prepared for undulating and hilly landscapes with demanding climbs, which are typical for this region of central Portugal.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific weather data for this route isn't provided, central Portugal generally offers pleasant cycling conditions in the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). These seasons typically avoid the extreme heat of summer and the colder, wetter days of winter, providing a more comfortable experience for challenging rides.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or scenic viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You can pass by the Igreja Matriz de Oliveira do Bairro, the tranquil Ribeira de Mortágua, and the historic Fountain from 1844. Other highlights include Visconde de Seabra Square and the Chapel of Saint Sebastian. The demanding climbs also promise rewarding panoramic views of the surrounding countryside.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to access the route in Oliveira do Bairro?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, Oliveira do Bairro, as a starting point for a circular route, typically offers public parking options within the town. Look for designated parking zones near the town center or main attractions to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established paths. You will find it intersects with parts of the Portuguese Way of St. James, Caminhos de Fátima, and the Grande Rota do Bussaco.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling on paved surfaces. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, bringing a dog on a demanding 75.6 km road cycling route is not typically recommended due to the nature of the sport and the potential for traffic. It is not considered a dog-friendly trail in the traditional sense.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no special permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route. You can enjoy the trail freely.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque rural landscapes typical of central Portugal. The route traverses through natural environments, potentially including cork and eucalyptus forests, vineyards, and small country roads. It offers a tranquil setting with opportunities to see traditional towns and villages.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a challenging road cycling trip like this?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length (75.6 km, ~4 hours), it's essential to bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate cycling gear. A helmet is highly recommended. Consider carrying a first-aid kit and a fully charged phone for emergencies.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the route?
faqs.answer: While specific cafes or pubs are not listed, both Oliveira do Bairro and Mortágua are towns where you can find amenities. The route also passes through rural areas, so it's advisable to carry sufficient provisions and plan your stops in the main towns.
